critical.message: eval() executes arbitrary code — extremely dangerous
critical.fix: Never use eval() with user input. Find an alternative approach.

critical.message: document.write() can execute malicious scripts (XSS)
critical.fix: Use DOM manipulation methods like createElement() or textContent instead.

critical.message: Setting innerHTML directly can cause XSS attacks
critical.fix: Use textContent for plain text, or sanitize HTML with DOMPurify before using innerHTML.

critical.message: Setting innerHTML directly can cause XSS attacks
critical.fix: Use textContent for plain text, or sanitize HTML with DOMPurify before using innerHTML.

critical.message: dangerouslySetInnerHTML is a React XSS risk
critical.fix: Sanitize content with DOMPurify before passing to dangerouslySetInnerHTML.

critical.message: child_process execution detected — potential command injection
critical.fix: Validate and sanitize all inputs. Never build shell commands from user input.

critical.message: Synchronous shell execution — command injection risk
critical.fix: Use execFile() with an arguments array instead of exec() with a string.

critical.message: Shell command execution — potential command injection
critical.fix: Use execFile() with an arguments array. Never pass user input to exec().

critical.message: Function() constructor is similar to eval() — code injection risk
critical.fix: Avoid using the Function() constructor with dynamic strings.

critical.message: new Function() executes arbitrary code — code injection risk
critical.fix: Avoid using new Function() with user-controlled input.

critical.message: Unsafe deserialization detected — remote code execution risk
critical.fix: Use JSON.parse() for safe deserialization instead of third-party deserialize().

critical.message: vm.runInNewContext() can execute arbitrary code
critical.fix: Avoid executing dynamic code. Use safer alternatives.

critical.message: vm.runInThisContext() executes arbitrary code
critical.fix: Avoid vm module for dynamic code execution with user input.

critical.message: Setting outerHTML can cause XSS vulnerabilities
critical.fix: Use DOM methods instead of setting outerHTML with user data.

critical.message: insertAdjacentHTML with user input causes XSS
critical.fix: Sanitize input with DOMPurify before using insertAdjacentHTML.

critical.message: setTimeout with string argument acts like eval()
critical.fix: Pass a function reference instead: setTimeout(() => doSomething(), delay)

critical.message: setInterval with string argument acts like eval()
critical.fix: Pass a function reference instead: setInterval(() => doSomething(), delay)

critical.message: Synchronous shell spawn — command injection risk
critical.fix: Use spawn() async with validated arguments array.

critical.message: Node vm module used — potential sandbox escape
critical.fix: Avoid executing dynamic code from user input via vm module.

high.message: child_process module imported — potential command injection
high.fix: Validate all inputs strictly before passing to any child_process methods.

high.message: child_process module imported — potential command injection
high.fix: Validate all inputs strictly before passing to any child_process methods.

high.message: shell:true in subprocess allows shell injection attacks
high.fix: Use shell: false and pass arguments as an array.

high.message: HTTP server created — ensure proper input validation and rate limiting
high.fix: Add helmet.js, rate limiting, and input validation middleware.

high.message: File write operation — path traversal risk if using user input
high.fix: Validate and sanitize file paths. Use path.basename() to prevent directory traversal.

high.message: File read operation — path traversal risk if using user input
high.fix: Validate file paths against a whitelist of allowed directories.

high.message: Cookie being set — ensure secure flags are set
high.fix: Set httpOnly: true, secure: true, and sameSite: 'strict' on all cookies.

high.message: JWT verify without error handling can cause authentication bypass
high.fix: Always wrap jwt.verify() in try/catch and handle errors properly.

high.message: SQL string concatenation detected — SQL injection risk
high.fix: Use parameterized queries or an ORM instead of string concatenation.

high.message: SQL query concatenation — SQL injection risk
high.fix: Use prepared statements with parameterized queries.

high.message: SQL injection vulnerability — user input in query
high.fix: Use parameterized queries: db.query('WHERE id = ?', [userId])

high.message: SQL injection vulnerability — user input in query
high.fix: Use parameterized queries: db.query('WHERE id = ?', [userId])

high.message: User input passed directly to database query
high.fix: Sanitize and validate all user inputs before database operations.

high.message: File deletion with potential path traversal
high.fix: Validate file paths strictly before deletion operations.

high.message: Directory removal — potential path traversal
high.fix: Validate paths and ensure operations are within allowed directories.

high.message: User input used in file path — path traversal risk
high.fix: Use path.basename() and validate against allowed directory.

high.message: Prototype pollution vulnerability detected
high.fix: Use Object.create(null) for dictionaries, validate keys, use Map instead.

high.message: Potential prototype pollution via constructor
high.fix: Sanitize object keys and avoid setting constructor properties from user input.

high.message: Ensure helmet is configured properly
high.fix: Use helmet with CSP: app.use(helmet({ contentSecurityPolicy: {...} }))

high.message: Use helmet instead of manually disabling headers
high.fix: Use helmet.js to set all security headers automatically.

high.message: CORS reflecting all origins — security risk
high.fix: Whitelist specific origins: cors({ origin: process.env.CLIENT_URL })

high.message: User input reflected in response — potential XSS
high.fix: Escape user input before sending in response.

high.message: Open redirect vulnerability — user controls redirect URL
high.fix: Validate redirect URLs against a whitelist of allowed destinations.

high.message: JWT signing — ensure strong algorithm and secret
high.fix: Use RS256 algorithm and strong secret. Set expiry: jwt.sign(payload, secret, { expiresIn: '1h' })

high.message: JWT with no algorithm — authentication bypass risk
high.fix: Never use 'none' algorithm. Use RS256 or HS256 with a strong secret.

medium.message: Math.random() is not cryptographically secure
medium.fix: Use crypto.randomBytes() or crypto.randomUUID() for security-sensitive operations.

medium.message: Insecure HTTP connection — data transmitted in plaintext
medium.fix: Use HTTPS for all connections to encrypt data in transit.

medium.message: Possible password being logged to console
medium.fix: Never log sensitive data like passwords, tokens, or PII.

medium.message: Possible token being logged to console
medium.fix: Never log authentication tokens or credentials.

medium.message: Possible secret being logged to console
medium.fix: Never log secrets or sensitive data to console.

medium.message: Sensitive data may be stored in localStorage — accessible via XSS
medium.fix: Never store tokens or sensitive data in localStorage. Use httpOnly cookies instead.

medium.message: Sensitive data may be stored in sessionStorage — accessible via XSS
medium.fix: Use httpOnly cookies for sensitive session data instead of sessionStorage.

medium.message: CORS wildcard allows any origin to access your API
medium.fix: Specify allowed origins explicitly: origin: process.env.CLIENT_URL

medium.message: CORS wildcard (*) — any website can call your API
medium.fix: Restrict CORS to specific trusted origins only.

medium.message: MD5 is a cryptographically broken hashing algorithm
medium.fix: Use bcrypt for passwords or SHA-256 for data integrity checks.

medium.message: MD5 hashing is cryptographically weak
medium.fix: Use crypto.createHash('sha256') or bcrypt for passwords.

medium.message: SHA1 is deprecated and cryptographically weak
medium.fix: Use crypto.createHash('sha256') or stronger.

medium.message: Empty catch block silently swallows errors
medium.fix: Log errors or handle them properly. Never silently ignore exceptions.

medium.message: Empty catch block — errors are silently ignored
medium.fix: Add error logging: catch(err) { logger.error(err.message); }

medium.message: Empty catch block silently swallows errors
medium.fix: Handle or log all caught exceptions.

medium.message: Development-only code path detected
medium.fix: Ensure this code path is not reachable in production.

medium.message: Static file serving — ensure no sensitive files are exposed
medium.fix: Configure a .gitignore-style blocklist to prevent serving .env or config files.

medium.message: File upload handler — validate file types and size
medium.fix: Add file type whitelist, size limits, and virus scanning for uploads.

medium.message: Full error object sent to client — may expose stack traces
medium.fix: Send generic error messages to clients. Log detailed errors server-side only.

medium.message: Error details sent to client — information disclosure
medium.fix: Never send raw error objects to clients. Use generic error messages.

medium.message: parseInt on user input without radix
medium.fix: Always specify radix: parseInt(req.params.id, 10)

medium.message: User input used in RegExp — ReDoS vulnerability
medium.fix: Never create RegExp from user input. Use a regex library with timeout support.

medium.message: Dynamic RegExp — potential ReDoS if using user input
medium.fix: Validate and sanitize input before using in RegExp.

medium.message: Object serialization — ensure no sensitive data included
medium.fix: Explicitly whitelist fields to serialize rather than serializing entire objects.

medium.message: Parsing user-supplied JSON — validate schema after parsing
medium.fix: Use a JSON schema validator like joi or zod after parsing.

low.fix: Implement authentication before deploying to production.

low.message: Validation being skipped — potential security risk
low.fix: Always validate and sanitize user input.
Enable validation for all user inputs.

low.message: Use of var — prefer const/let for block scoping
low.fix: Replace var with const (immutable) or let (mutable) for proper scoping.

low.message: Loose equality with null — use strict equality
low.fix: Use === null for strict null checks.

low.message: Loose equality with undefined — use strict equality
low.fix: Use === undefined or optional chaining: value?.property
Remove console.log statements or replace with a proper logger like winston.
Remove all debugger statements before committing.
Complete or create a ticket for this TODO before merging.
Fix this issue before merging to main branch.

low.message: HACK comment indicates a workaround that needs proper fix
low.fix: Replace this hack with a proper solution.
Use specific types or 'unknown' with type guards instead of 'any'.

low.message: TypeScript 'any' type annotation bypasses type safety
low.fix: Define a proper interface or type instead of using 'any'.
Fix the TypeScript error instead of suppressing it.
Fix the underlying issue instead of disabling the linting rule.
Implement proper functionality or remove the no-op.

critical.message: eval() executes arbitrary Python code — extremely dangerous
critical.fix: Never use eval() with user input. Use ast.literal_eval() for safe evaluation of literals.

critical.message: exec() executes arbitrary Python code — extremely dangerous
critical.fix: Avoid exec() entirely. Find a safer alternative approach.

critical.message: pickle.loads() can execute arbitrary code during deserialization
critical.fix: Use JSON for data serialization. Never deserialize pickle from untrusted sources.

critical.message: pickle.load() can execute arbitrary code during deserialization
critical.fix: Use JSON for data serialization. Never load pickle from untrusted files.

critical.message: Dynamic import with __import__() — potential code injection
critical.fix: Use importlib.import_module() with a strict whitelist of allowed modules.

critical.message: marshal.loads() can execute arbitrary code
critical.fix: Avoid marshal for deserialization of untrusted data. Use JSON instead.

critical.message: compile() with user input allows code injection
critical.fix: Never compile user-controlled strings as Python code.

critical.message: Dynamic import from user input — code injection risk
critical.fix: Whitelist allowed module names before importing.

critical.message: shelve uses pickle internally — deserialization risk
critical.fix: Use a database or JSON storage instead of shelve for untrusted data.

critical.message: dill.loads() can execute arbitrary code like pickle
critical.fix: Never deserialize dill data from untrusted sources.

high.message: subprocess.call with shell=True — command injection risk
high.fix: Use shell=False and pass arguments as a list: subprocess.call(['cmd', 'arg'])

high.message: subprocess.run with shell=True — command injection risk
high.fix: Use shell=False and pass arguments as a list: subprocess.run(['cmd', 'arg'])

high.message: subprocess.Popen with shell=True — command injection risk
high.fix: Use shell=False and pass arguments as a list.

high.message: os.system() executes shell commands — command injection risk
high.fix: Use subprocess.run() with shell=False and an arguments list instead.

high.message: os.popen() executes shell commands — command injection risk
high.fix: Use subprocess.run() with shell=False instead.

high.message: yaml.load() without Loader is unsafe — arbitrary code execution
high.fix: Use yaml.safe_load() instead of yaml.load() for untrusted input.

high.message: Flask request args used — ensure input is validated
high.fix: Validate and sanitize all request parameters before use.

high.message: f-string in SQL query — SQL injection vulnerability
high.fix: Use parameterized queries: cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM t WHERE id = %s', (id,))

high.message: Raw SQL string execution — SQL injection risk
high.fix: Use parameterized queries with %s placeholders.

high.message: User input concatenated into string — injection risk
high.fix: Validate and sanitize request.args before use.

high.message: SQL query built with f-string — injection vulnerability
high.fix: Use ORM methods or parameterized queries instead.

high.message: User input used in file open — path traversal risk
high.fix: Validate file paths against a whitelist of allowed directories.

high.message: User input in file path — path traversal risk
high.fix: Use os.path.basename() and validate against allowed directory.

high.message: User-controlled file path in send_file — path traversal
high.fix: Validate file path against allowed directory before sending.

high.message: Dynamic Jinja2 template from user input — SSTI risk
high.fix: Never render user-controlled strings as templates.

high.message: Server-side template injection — critical vulnerability
high.fix: Never pass user input to render_template_string().

high.message: XML-RPC is vulnerable to XML injection attacks
high.fix: Use REST/JSON APIs instead of XML-RPC where possible.

high.message: XML parsing — XXE vulnerability if DTD processing enabled
high.fix: Disable DTD processing: parser = etree.XMLParser(resolve_entities=False)

high.message: XML parsing from untrusted input — XXE risk
high.fix: Use defusedxml library for safe XML parsing.

medium.message: random.random() is not cryptographically secure
medium.fix: Use the secrets module for security-sensitive random values: secrets.token_hex()

medium.message: random.randint() is not cryptographically secure
medium.fix: Use secrets.randbelow() for security-sensitive random integers.

medium.message: MD5 is cryptographically broken
medium.fix: Use hashlib.sha256() for data integrity or bcrypt/argon2 for passwords.

medium.message: SHA1 is deprecated and cryptographically weak
medium.fix: Use hashlib.sha256() or stronger hashing algorithm.

medium.message: Python built-in HTTP server is not production-safe
medium.fix: Use a production WSGI server like gunicorn or uvicorn.

medium.message: Django/Flask debug mode is enabled — never use in production
medium.fix: Set DEBUG = False in production and use environment variables.

medium.message: Django ALLOWED_HOSTS wildcard — accepts requests from any host
medium.fix: Specify exact allowed hostnames: ALLOWED_HOSTS = ['yourdomain.com']

medium.message: SSL certificate verification disabled
medium.fix: Always verify SSL certificates. Remove verify=False and fix the certificate issue.

medium.message: Bare except clause catches all exceptions including SystemExit
medium.fix: Specify exception types: except Exception as e: or except (TypeError, ValueError) as e:

medium.message: Overly broad exception handler
medium.fix: Catch specific exceptions: except (ValueError, KeyError) as e:

medium.message: Bare pass in exception handler silently swallows errors
medium.fix: Log the error: logger.error(f'Error: {e}') instead of just pass.

medium.message: Assert statements are disabled with -O flag — don't use for security
medium.fix: Use explicit if/raise for security checks instead of assert.

medium.message: input() reads raw user input — validate before use
medium.fix: Always validate and sanitize input() results before use.

medium.message: HTTP request — ensure URL is not user-controlled
medium.fix: Validate URLs against a whitelist before making requests (SSRF prevention).

medium.message: HTTP POST request — validate target URL (SSRF risk)
medium.fix: Whitelist allowed URLs before making server-side requests.

medium.message: Flask-CORS without restrictions — all origins allowed
medium.fix: Restrict origins: CORS(app, origins=['https://yourdomain.com'])

medium.message: JWT decode — ensure algorithm is specified
medium.fix: Always specify algorithms: jwt.decode(token, key, algorithms=['HS256'])

medium.message: Debug logging in production may expose sensitive data
medium.fix: Use logging.INFO or logging.WARNING in production.

medium.message: Printing request data — may log sensitive information
medium.fix: Use proper logging with appropriate log levels and data sanitization.

low.message: Possible password being printed to stdout
low.fix: Never print sensitive data like passwords or tokens.

low.message: Possible token being printed to stdout
low.fix: Never print authentication tokens or credentials.

low.message: Possible secret being printed to stdout
low.fix: Never print secrets or sensitive configuration values.
Implement this block before deploying to production.
Complete or create a ticket for this TODO before merging.
Fix this issue before merging to main branch.

low.message: Global variable usage — reduces testability and can cause bugs
low.fix: Avoid global state. Pass variables as parameters or use class attributes.

low.message: Mutable default argument — shared between all function calls
low.fix: Use None as default and initialize inside function: def f(items=None): items = items or []

low.message: Mutable list as default argument — shared state bug
low.fix: Use None default: def f(items=None): items = items or []

low.message: Mutable dict as default argument — shared state bug
low.fix: Use None default: def f(items=None): items = items or {}

low.message: Use isinstance() instead of type() for type checking
low.fix: Use isinstance(x, ExpectedType) for proper type checking.
Implement proper function logic or use a named function.
Fix the underlying issue instead of suppressing the linting warning.
Fix the underlying issue instead of disabling the pylint check.

secrets.message: Hardcoded password detected in source code
secrets.fix: Use environment variables: os.getenv('PASSWORD') or process.env.PASSWORD

secrets.message: Hardcoded password detected in source code
secrets.fix: Use environment variables: os.getenv('PASSWORD') or process.env.PASSWORD

secrets.message: Hardcoded password detected in source code
secrets.fix: Use environment variables and never commit secrets to version control.

secrets.message: Hardcoded API key detected
secrets.fix: Store API keys in environment variables and use .env files (add to .gitignore).

secrets.message: Hardcoded API key detected
secrets.fix: Store API keys in environment variables and use .env files (add to .gitignore).

secrets.message: Hardcoded API key detected
secrets.fix: Store API keys in environment variables and use .env files (add to .gitignore).

secrets.message: Hardcoded secret detected
secrets.fix: Move secrets to environment variables immediately.

secrets.message: Hardcoded secret detected
secrets.fix: Move secrets to environment variables immediately.

secrets.message: Hardcoded secret key detected
secrets.fix: Generate a random secret key and store it in environment variables.

secrets.message: Hardcoded JWT secret detected
secrets.fix: Store JWT secret in environment variables: process.env.JWT_SECRET

secrets.message: Hardcoded token detected
secrets.fix: Store tokens in environment variables, never in source code.

secrets.message: Hardcoded access token detected
secrets.fix: Store access tokens in environment variables.

secrets.message: Hardcoded GitHub token detected
secrets.fix: Store GitHub tokens in environment variables and revoke the exposed token immediately.

secrets.message: Hardcoded MongoDB connection string detected
secrets.fix: Store database URIs in environment variables: process.env.MONGO_URI

secrets.message: MongoDB Atlas connection string with credentials in code
secrets.fix: Move connection string to environment variables immediately.

secrets.message: AWS Access Key ID detected in source code
secrets.fix: Revoke this key immediately. Use IAM roles or environment variables.

secrets.message: AWS Secret Access Key detected in source code
secrets.fix: Revoke this key immediately. Use IAM roles or AWS Secrets Manager.

secrets.message: Stripe secret key detected — payment data at risk
secrets.fix: Revoke this key immediately in your Stripe dashboard and use environment variables.

secrets.message: Stripe live secret key detected — CRITICAL: payment data exposed
secrets.fix: Revoke immediately at dashboard.stripe.com. Use environment variables.

secrets.message: Stripe test secret key detected in source code
secrets.fix: Even test keys should not be committed. Use environment variables.

secrets.message: SendGrid API key detected
secrets.fix: Store API keys in environment variables and revoke the exposed key.

secrets.message: Twilio auth token detected in source code
secrets.fix: Revoke immediately at twilio.com. Store in environment variables.

secrets.message: Private key reference detected in source code
secrets.fix: Store private keys in secure key management systems, never in source code.

secrets.message: RSA Private key embedded in source code — CRITICAL
secrets.fix: Remove immediately. Store private keys in secure file storage outside the repo.

secrets.message: Private key embedded in source code — CRITICAL
secrets.fix: Remove immediately. Store private keys securely outside the repository.

secrets.message: OAuth client secret detected in source code
secrets.fix: Store OAuth secrets in environment variables: process.env.GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET

secrets.message: Google OAuth client secret detected
secrets.fix: Store in environment variables and regenerate in Google Cloud Console.

secrets.message: Firebase private key detected in source code
secrets.fix: Store Firebase credentials in environment variables or use service account JSON securely.

secrets.message: OpenAI API key detected in source code
secrets.fix: Store in environment variables immediately. Revoke and regenerate at platform.openai.com.

secrets.message: Anthropic API key detected in source code
secrets.fix: Store in environment variables and revoke the exposed key.

secrets.message: Redis password hardcoded in source code
secrets.fix: Use environment variables: process.env.REDIS_PASSWORD

secrets.message: Database URL may contain credentials
secrets.fix: Store DATABASE_URL in environment variables, never commit to version control.

secrets.message: PostgreSQL password hardcoded
secrets.fix: Use environment variables for all database credentials.

secrets.message: MySQL root password hardcoded
secrets.fix: Use environment variables: process.env.MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD

secrets.message: Hardcoded database connection string
secrets.fix: Store connection strings in environment variables.

secrets.message: JWT token hardcoded in source code
secrets.fix: Never hardcode tokens. Use environment variables or token refresh flows.

secrets.message: GitHub Personal Access Token detected
secrets.fix: Revoke this token immediately at github.com/settings/tokens and use environment variables.

secrets.message: GitHub OAuth token detected in source code
secrets.fix: Revoke this token and store OAuth tokens securely.

secrets.message: Slack Bot token detected in source code
secrets.fix: Revoke at api.slack.com and store in environment variables.

secrets.message: Slack User token detected in source code
secrets.fix: Revoke at api.slack.com and store in environment variables.

secrets.message: Mailgun API key detected
secrets.fix: Store in environment variables and revoke the exposed key at mailgun.com.

secrets.message: Cloudinary API secret detected
secrets.fix: Store in environment variables. Revoke at cloudinary.com/console.

secrets.message: Heroku API key detected in source code
secrets.fix: Revoke at dashboard.heroku.com and store in environment variables.
